findings.description: Page 2 logos and graphics are not preserved in their original grouped layout.
findings.evidence: In the PDF, the 'Our Role' slide shows two large agency logos at the top, two website screenshots beneath, and a structured grid of partner logos across the bottom. In the HTML, many logos are broken out into a loose vertical sequence with large empty gaps, and the intended grouped composition is lost.

findings.description: Page 2 overall layout structure is significantly altered.
findings.evidence: The PDF uses a balanced slide layout with centered heading and multiple rows of aligned graphics/logos. The HTML renders content as a long, scattered single-column/offset arrangement with substantial whitespace, changing the page's visual organization.

findings.description: Page 2 reading order is disrupted by scattered placement of logos and screenshots.
findings.evidence: In the PDF, viewers read the heading, then top logos, then screenshots, then partner logos. In the HTML, logos appear in a fragmented sequence before and after the screenshot, making the visual flow inconsistent with the source.

findings.description: Page 3 layout is simplified, with reduced emphasis on the title/URL area.
findings.evidence: The PDF presents a large centered title and URL above a large screenshot. The HTML preserves the same section ordering but uses smaller text and a more document-like layout rather than slide-style composition.

findings.description: Page 3 heading and URL formatting lose some visual prominence.
findings.evidence: The PDF uses large, bold, centered title text and prominent green URL text. The HTML shows the same text content but with smaller, left-aligned styling that weakens hierarchy.

findings.description: Page 4 two-part composition is simplified in HTML.
findings.evidence: The PDF clearly balances a form screenshot on the left with key takeaways and logos on the right. The HTML keeps the same content order but stacks or spaces elements more loosely, reducing the side-by-side structure.

findings.description: Page 4 text emphasis is reduced, including highlighted bullet treatment.
findings.evidence: In the PDF, 'Photo of plant for ID' is visibly highlighted in green and the takeaways are large slide bullets. In the HTML, the bullet list appears as standard text without the same strong emphasis.

findings.description: Page 5 image comparison layout is partially collapsed.
findings.evidence: The PDF presents a clear left-right comparison with good examples in a green-framed block and bad examples in a red-framed block. The HTML shows the same images but largely stacked and partially cropped across the page, weakening the comparison structure.

findings.description: Page 5 images are present but their comparative framing and placement are not fully preserved.
findings.evidence: The PDF uses green and red bordered panels to distinguish acceptable vs unacceptable photos. In the HTML, images appear separately and the strong panel-based visual grouping is diminished.

findings.description: Page 6 contact page layout is simplified from a two-area slide to a document-like flow.
findings.evidence: The PDF has a large centered title, contact text on the left, and a poster graphic on the right. The HTML preserves all content but places it in a more linear layout with less visual balance.

findings.description: Page 6 text formatting loses some hierarchy and color emphasis.
findings.evidence: The PDF uses large colored email addresses and a prominent title. The HTML shows the same text content but with smaller, plainer formatting, making headings and emails less visually distinct.

findings.description: Significant content from the 'Our Role' slide is missing. The HTML preserves logos and two screenshot images but omits several organization names visible as text/logos on the slide, including 'UofSC South Carolina' and 'Lake James Environmental Association.'
findings.evidence: PDF slide 2 shows additional partner logos/text such as 'UofSC South Carolina' and 'Lake James Environmental Association' along the bottom row. HTML section 'Our Role' includes img alts for many logos but no corresponding entry for 'UofSC South Carolina' or 'Lake James Environmental Association'.

findings.description: A notable text phrase is altered in meaning on slide 4: 'This information is communicated to the appropriate state resource agencies for incorporation into management plan' drops the article and may truncate the original wording.
findings.evidence: PDF slide 4 text reads: 'This information is communicated to the appropriate state resource agencies for incorporation into management plan' as visible on the slide; HTML reproduces the same phrase, but the surrounding layout suggests this slide was partially reconstructed and may not preserve exact original lineation. This is a weak issue compared with missing logo text, but slide 4 content is not captured with full fidelity.
